page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU core.

This instance type does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention from neighboring tenants. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio allows workloads requiring substantial memory footprints to run on a moderate core count, optimizing resource utilization.

The c4a-highmem-8 instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require GPU acceleration or local physical storage. Suitable use cases include medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that benefit from a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance within the c4a family, configured with 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration delivers a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core, making it suitable for memory-intensive operations. The instance features dedicated CPU allocation to ensure predictable performance, though it does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource balance provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require significant memory capacity without needing a high number of compute cores. The c4a-highmem-8 is designed for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching systems, data processing pipelines, and enterprise applications.
